lines.x: 3:23 P.M. \ Clear
Underneath a nondescript oak tree, outside the nomadic town of Wolumonde, Leithanien
Schultz Severin... This is your third pack of the day.
Schultz?
Oh, sorry, Uncle...
I need my tobacco. You're right, of course. Alcohol and tobacco are bad habits. Don't be like me.
But your cough keeps getting worse.
We're off the clock. No need to be so formal. How many times have I told you? Never mind.
He looks away. The burned tents, the weeds growing out of control. He flicks the ash from his cigarette.
He hoped to find some peace and quiet in his solitude, even if it was just a change of his title.
*Sigh*... They made you come all the way out here!
The bunch of old schemers. They won't even come to me themselves. They send out a little girl to get hurt——*Cough* *cough*, *cough* *cough*!
Calm down, Uncle! I volunteered. I thought it would be better to tell you myself.
*Wheeze*——*cough* *cough*, *cough* *cough*——
You really have to quit smoking and drinking. Look how bad you've gotten...
I've more or less figured it out. Damn it.
Well, there was a heated discussion back in council. The young people hid in the parlor. No one dared to speak.
No, no one helped me. I couldn't convince anyone on my own. I'm sorry.
The situation has been tense since the town went off course. We don't need to be standing on ceremony anymore. Not that anyone would listen to students and kids, even if they did speak up.
And then what happened?
The councilors agreed that because it was impossible to distinguish between Infected and the state of their infection, it's better safe than sorry...
The bodies need to be buried outside the town. And of course... no funerals.
*Sigh*...
I... I'm sorry...
But some of them are people of Wolumonde. Our own. We can't just abandon them.
Eckehard was the best tailor in town. He took over the shop from his father. We wouldn't have weddings without him.
And poor Biederman. He lost his job as a Catastrophe Messenger because of the Great Rift. But he's trying to atone. He is strong.
Kevin wasn't Infected. He worked hard for his wife. He was a good husband.
And Thorwald, he——*cough* *cough*, *hack* *cough*——
No more smoking, Schultz. Thor even said so.
Can you accept it, child? Honestly? Forget what the old schemers think.
I... I don't know...
I just want Thor to be buried so he can rest in peace. I don't want people treating him like he's a threat when he's already dead... to be tossed out there... to the elements.
Alright.
Why do we have to treat them like that? We never did anything like that before.
And that Oripathy doctor. She was incredible. She refused to abandon Wolumonde. All four of them have the right to a decent burial, but——
Putting them together with the Infected——
We should just be blaming the fire.
I know.
But... But let's save that for later. For now, we can't just dump their remains.
I contacted the nearest Gendarmerie, and they will have experts on the rescue team to assist with the investigation. Until then, we'll keep the bodies where I can care for them.
Once we have specialists on scene, we can treat the dead with appropriate care.
We can still get help?
They could be a month out, depending on the exact length of the rift. It could honestly be several months.
Wolumonde is getting worse and worse every day now that we're off course. Everyone is saying we'll have to head around the whole Great Rift to get back on track.
But if the Great Rift is that big, if the detour is too long...
Anyway, we have to wait.
Give me a minute with Thor. I think... I think I'm entitled to that.
If the old schemers want to leave my boy in the middle of nowhere, they'll have to shoot their own Schultz.
They'll have to shoot me.
They won't! We all know how much you've done for the town!
...No, forget I said that.
I can't say that at a time like this. The Schultz can't talk like that. We have work to do.
But Thor is still your son!
Tatjana, you go back and tell them. Tell them...
Tell them...
Tell them I agree. *Cough*. I agree with them.
*Cough* *cough* cough*, *cough* *cough*——
They came back.
Hm.
Thanks to the locals, we were able to slip into the nearby town and replenish our supplies.
The hunters will be back soon. Hope we get something today. I'm sick of sandworms. Two days is enough.
Hm.
How about water?
Not an issue. Gotta thank them for that too.
But seriously, I don't like talking to them. I always feel like they're looking down on me.
Hm.
It looks like Wolumonde is going to start migrating again soon. We have to see some results by then.
We have help. It will be good.
Help...? Maybe.
But I don't trust the local Infected. They're just out for revenge.
Leithanien is better to the Infected than most places are. And since everyone here uses Arts, they don't have much reason to fear or persecute them.
And isn't it enough that the Infected can have a decent life here without being sent straight to the gallows?
I think they're just a bunch of thugs, stealing our name for their own purposes—
Name?
What's in... a name?
Erm...
We don't trust them... fine. But... who do we trust? Who should we trust?
Can you stop talking so creepily? Whatever you say, captain.
...Thank you.
Oh, a leaf. It fell on your head...
Huh? What?
A few boxes of dried meat. Fresh-picked fruit. Water. It's early autumn. Still a long way to winter...
We have time.
Okay. You're still kind of confusing me with that talk.
Anyway, it's your call when to start the war.
War? No...
Huh?
Look. The hunters are back.
Seems our food will be better tonight.
I hope so. We should eat more meat while we can. Food is going to be a big problem come winter.
Did Bob ever write back?
Haven't heard from him since we got to Leithanien... Erm, by the way, I've been meaning to ask...
Like, was his code name 'Bob' and they started calling him 'Big Bob' because he's a big guy, or was he 'Big Bob' from the start?
...I don't remember.
Whatever. They're bounty hunters. Maybe they found the treasure.
Treasure. Hm.
It would be nice... if there was a treasure to help us too.
It's been 27 days!
How could she take so long to make contact? She's obsessed with punctuality!
Atro... where are you...?
Folinic?
Oh, Lisa... Err, I'm supposed to call you Operator Suzuran now, huh?
It's a good code name.
Hehe, thanks!
You've been watching the communication room for 20 hours now. Aren't you going to take a break?
Has it been that long?
Yes! So please go to the break room and get some rest. I'll take over for you.
Okay... It's really been that long...
Still no word from Atro?
None yet. I already put in an application to go to Leithanien. If Amiya approves, I'll leave right away.
Leithanien? Don't you have work to do?
She's my classmate, and my best friend. I can't... I won't abandon her.
Atro's last contact was with the office in northern Leithanien. Unless something big happened, she wouldn't go far from there.
Then I'll go too!
No.
Huh?! Harsh...
You- you just want to find Atro, right? It won't be dangerous?
Who knows what the situation could be.
That's okay! I'm a fully accredited operator now, and a student of Dr. Kal'tsit!
And I can't stand seeing my big sister Folinic's worried face all the time! I have to do something!
*Sigh*. I never imagined little Lisa would worry about me someday. Everything's all out of order lately.
Big or small, we all have worries.
Well, okay! I reported in to the local branch office anyway. If anything goes wrong, they'll send help.
Let's just wait for Amiya's approval and then we'll go together. But you'll have to stay close by me.
A message? Is it Atro?
No. It's a brief, sent by an operator in Leithanien who received our message.
Folinic?
Atro was spotted in a small town in Leithanien... And about one month ago...
That town suffered a Catastrophe.
2:10 P.M. \ Clear
Wolumonde, Leithanien. Once a bustling hub of commerce, this nomadic town is now rarely seen by outsiders.
Rathaus
Schultz Severin Hawthorn, where have you been?
How are we supposed to get anything done in a strategic planning session without you?
Was I here when you decided my son's fate?
Well... Tatjana should already have...
Yes, she told me. You had her tell me. I'm only reminding you what you've done.
What's the situation?
The Infected are still protesting. They've even started chasing off Infected newcomers.
What do they want?
They... they believe the fire was part of some kind of plot. They demand an explanation for Dr. Atro and the dead Infected.
A plot? An explanation? Great, someone must be riling them up.
Even the Infected wouldn't stoop that low. Most of them are from Wolumonde.
Have they had any contact with that militant group out there?
None, for now.
But the militants have been lingering around the outskirts of the hamlet. I'm worried they might influence the protestors.
Just a second, it's from the sentry—
Go ahead.
Two? Infected?
They claim to be from Rhodes Island? I know that name—
That would be Dr. Atro's company. *Sigh.* We knew this was coming.
Wh-what do we do?
Dr. Atro was a respectable Infected. They may be here looking for her. And they have a right to know the truth.
Let them in.
...You heard him. Do as he says.
Hmm, are you sure about this? What if they have something to do with the militants out there?
Between the Great Rift, the fire, and the Infected, do we have the luxury to be suspicious of everyone who knocks on our door?
What about after they learn the truth? What if Rhodes Island seeks reparations from us? How much is Wolumonde to suffer?!
...So what should we do?
Lock our doors and send them away? Lie to them? Tell them Dr. Atro took some Oripathy patients out on a hike?
I know we can't sweep it under the rug. But you don't have the final say in this. We have to prepare for the worst.
No one has the final say on the truth, but... Never mind. Do as you will.
I'm leaving.
Leaving? Where are you going this time?
...I have a problem to solve.
My only job is to get this town back on track. The decision-making process? I have little patience for your political games.
Hey! Wait!
I know you're suffering, and there's so much we could've done better, but we had no choice!
I understand. That's why I'm still talking to you nice and peacefully.
I'll leave Rhodes Island to you. You're good at these things. I'll walk the blocks.
That's the deal.
Severin! Severin Hawthorn! You are this town's Schultz! You can't be running all over the—
Ugh! Must he always...
Sigh...
Forget it. Tatjana, Severin, Thor. They're all tragic figures, really.
Let's get this place cleaned up. Rhodes Island will be here soon.
Wolumonde. Its name means 'The Eighth Moon,' and together with seven other nearby towns, it makes up Leithanien's flourishing northern trade network—
That's what the book said, anyway.
From what I'm seeing, 'flourishing trade' isn't exactly how I'd describe it.
Could it be because of the 'Great Rift' thing?
Yes. A Catastrophe of that scale is rare. It could be more than a thousand meters deep, with exposed active Originium on the cliffsides. I shudder just thinking about it.
But, Miss Folinic... You were very curious what would happen if you lobbed a shell into it...
Nay, that was just my academic curiosity speaking. Realistically, one wrong move and the entire plateau could get cleaved in half.
Considering the detour we had to take, looks like this town won't be moving an inch.
Visitors at a time like this? And such strange clothes. I hope this isn't an ill omen...
Look at her tails. Oh, and the crystals on her shoulder! Is she Infected?
But those clothes look familiar... Where have I seen them before...?
M-Miss Folinic, can we really just walk down the street like this?
The prevalence of Originium Arts in Leithanien have brought two major benefits: first, the incredible music scene, and second, a broad tolerance of the Infected.
There may be restrictions on your personal freedom, but should you want to live, you can win back that right as long as you're willing to pay the price.
...Price?
At least it's something affordable.
Wolumonde may be in the boonies and have its fair share of problems, but at least the Infected have a place to live... That's what she told me anyway—
—Oh, someone's coming.
(Hm? That man's uniform...)
(Is he with the Wolumonde Gendarmerie? Should we ask for directions?)
(He's coming toward us. Is this the welcoming committee?)
(I'm not sure...)
(Oh, he lit a cigarette.)
(He put his lighter into his pocket. Now he's holding the cigarette with his other hand. He switched hands again—)
(—He walked away without looking back! Miss Folinic!)
Excuse me—!
Hm.
Hello, we're—
Ah, Rhodes Island, I take it?
The Rathaus is just up ahead, right in front of the tallest tower. You see it? Good. Someone will meet you there.
Er, thanks. Can I ask your name...?
Me? I'm off duty.
Um, what?
I'm off duty. And I really don't want to talk so much when I'm off... I have my own business to deal with.
That's the deal. Later.
And he's gone?
He was kind of rude, but I think he's been through a lot...
Hah... as if we're not tired!
But at least he told us where to go. Let's go talk to the local—
Schultz! There you are!
The Infected are protesting again. The Gendarmerie are already on the scene, but they need you there leading them—
Ah! You're smoking! Did the shopkeepers get to you again? Please, you have an image to maintain. You wouldn't want to lose the trust of the people—
Hm? What is it?
...That's a... Schultz? A 'Schultheiß' is like a local leader?
Is something wrong, Schultz?
No. You're always working so hard, Tatjana. I admire you.
Do you? Thank you—
Now, about those two, they're not from around here, are they? Could they be the doctors from Rhodes Island?
Oh? Well, yes. We are indeed. I am Folinic and this is Suzuran.
(Doctors?)
(I think we count.)
(Even me?)
(I'm a fully-fledged operator assigned to the Medical Department. And Lisa, you've... studied with Dr. Kal'tsit, so we can call you a medical intern. I guess.)
I see... Oh! You two must be on your way to the Rathaus? I'm so sorry to get in your way!
Of course it should be the Schultz who receives you, but he really is needed at Zwölftontechnik Strasse... Er...
Oh, really, he can—
Ahem! Very good, Tatjana, you take them. I'll go look into this Infected situation.
You two.
—Yes?
I know why you're here. But our situation is dire.
Do you know what happened to Wolumonde?
The Great Rift.
Yes. I get to skip over so much when dealing with smart people.
So now the Infected are more riled up than ever before. If you don't want to get mixed up in that, stay away from Zwölftontechnik Strasse.
We are experts at dealing with the Infected.
I know. We had a Dr. Atro from your company working closely with us.
So you know we can help.
We don't need it yet. It wouldn't be right to drag our guests into this mess as soon as they've arrived. That would get us into trouble too.
...So what is Atro doing here?
Um...
She's not here right now.
So where is she?
She...
Oh no! Fighting has broken out!
Th-those Infected have weapons! What's going on?!
They've got blades! Are they going to kill us?
Schultz!
...I know.
You two are Infected experts, yes?
Come with me.
Wh-why are those outsiders sticking their noses in our business?!
And you, Severin! Your son died in that fire! How can you still aid those incompetent swine?!
...Put down the blade and tell me who pushed you to do this? Who gave you those weapons?
Final warning.
Gah—!
That's assault with a deadly weapon. You think you can just go back to your homes? You'll get a knock on your door soon enough. The Gendarmerie will hold you accountable.
Just 'hold them accountable?'
I don't have to explain our accountability processes to a couple of tourists.
Do you have anything at all to tell 'a couple of tourists' then? 'Schultz?'
I do. You're strong. Stronger than I'd expect a doctor to be. Impressive.
And... thank you for the assistance.
Usually, we go by 'Operator.'
Now can you tell us where Atro is?
It's not safe here. Let's talk somewhere else.
Good work, Miss Folinic.
Sorry I'm not much help in battle...
It's fine. You did your best. If I'd known what was happening in Wolumonde, I wouldn't have brought you along.
Even if we're just helping resolve some disputes, you're too young to be dragged into—
Miss Folinic!
—*Sigh* ...Fine, fine. Little Lisa's all grown up now. Did you get hurt?
No, I'm fine.
But you were spacing out back there. What's wrong?
The quarantine zone is right near here. Everything seems to have gone back to peace and quiet after we drove off the protestors.
Mm... It doesn't seem that different from any other place.
You're right, it's not that different. They have shops, cafés, ads for music halls and art salons...
No one's getting rounded up by guards and sent out to the tundra to die. They get to live pretty ordinary lives... You might even say they live well.
The Infected get treated so differently all around the world.
If every place was so good to the Infected...
We'd be out of a job.
Wha?! I mean, that's true, but I'd be really happy about it! E-even though I just became an operator...
I'm kidding... The fact that Infected can roam the streets freely only serves to underscore how special the situation in Wolumonde is.
...They're hiding something from us.
Huh? Where did that come from—
Shh! Lower your voice! We'll talk once the officer is gone!
The Infected rebels are acting really weird. And they keep talking about a 'fire' and a 'doctor,' right?
Add in that officer who definitely knows who we are, and who really doesn't want to tell me anything about Atro...
And I can't shake the feeling that something rotten is brewing in this town.
Even Leithanien wouldn't go so far as to let the Infected riot in the streets. Where are the guards? Why isn't anyone stopping them?
Now that you mention it...
I've got a bad feeling about this.
M-maybe everyone's just a little on edge? Considering what the town's been through...
...I pray that's all it is.
I'm so sorry to have dragged you into all this just as you've arrived. It's shameful...
Oh, don't worry about it.
Are you part of the local Gendarmerie?
Not really. But everyone in Wolumonde undergoes military training. When the time comes, we have a duty to take up arms...
But Uncle... Err, Schultz Severin is our town's highest official.
The middle-aged smoker who really doesn't want to do his job? Too bad, but that irresponsible disregard for duty doesn't exactly come off as mysterious.
There's a reason he is the way he is—
No... never mind...
Oh right, how should I call you two?
Call me Operator Folinic, and this is Operator Suzuran.
Folinic, Suzuran... these are code names?
Where we come from, most people prefer not to use their real names at work. Just like 'Atro.'
Dr. Atro...
Seems you're acquainted.
Yes... Sorry. I know you have your doubts, but the Schultz will give you the answers you need. It's not my place to say.
Now, come with me please.
Of course.
How much did you tell them?
Not much. Not enough to upset anyone.
I... I've put forward some unreasonable ideas, but you're always free to reject them.
That's a creative way to dodge responsibility. But you'll get no comfort from me.
If I stood here and told them the whole, unadulterated truth right now, would you be happy? Would that make everyone happy?
I represent the people, but you're the leader of Wolumonde. What does it matter if we're happy or not?
I don't want to make things difficult. Especially when I'm hosting guests at the same time the Infected are stirring.
*Sigh*... Put it away. You want to make a good impression on Rhodes Island. Now's not the time to be lighting cigarettes or playing with crossbow bolts.
I think we're well past any good impressions...
You——Wait, are they the two young girls out there?
Young girls? Hah, wait until you see them fight. Then you'll start wondering if Leithanian schools focus too much on the arts, just like those bureaucrats.
Sirs, they're here.
Ah, it's the doctors from Rhodes Island! Welcome to Wolumonde! I am so sorry you had to get roped into our local affairs like that. Let us make it up to you with an evening—
Thank you for the offer, but we're only here to establish Dr. Atro's whereabouts. We don't intend to stay long.
So, where is she?
Erm, well—
The local Infected seem to know 'Dr. Atro.' You're stalling, and I'd prefer you not.
Doctors, I'm very sorry, but things in Wolumonde are very complicated right now. We need some time to give your company a proper explanation, in order to guarantee—
She disappeared.
Wha?!
...Sir, when it comes to matters such as this, you might want to shed that flippant attitude of yours.
The truth is flippant. It's simple. No need to sugar coat it. You're angry now, but you're not surprised. You already guessed it, didn't you?
We received the Infected doctor who called herself 'Atro.' And we greatly appreciate her help caring for the many Infected residents of Wolumonde during this difficult time.
And you show your appreciation by telling us 'she disappeared?'
No need for hostility here... *Cough*, *hack hack*—
Sir, you look unwell. You should quit smoking.
It would probably be a good idea to get a physical examination, as it could be—
...No thanks, but I appreciate the concern.
Back to business, after Wolumonde steered over here to avoid Catastrophe, Dr. Atro set up camp on the edge of town. She was housing some Infected residents.
But not long ago, there was a fire in her camp.
Er...
...A fire?
I know what you're thinking. But according to what we currently know, it seemed to be an accident.
Dr. Atro left town after that. And we don't know where she went.
How can you act like this is all no big deal?!
Miss Folinic! C-calm down!
We... We'll investigate ourselves. But you tell us she just disappeared after a fire like that's not going to upset us!
...I'm very sorry.
If I'm not going to talk lightly, all I can give you is 'I'm very sorry.'
Dr. Atro is a good egg. She's got no Leithanian blood. No connection to us at all, really. And yet she's nobler than anybody around here.
And when the Catastrophe struck Wolumonde, she didn't run away. She offered us help.
I have the utmost respect for the good doctor. I hope nothing happened to her.
But after the fire, you saw it yourself, some of the Infected folks don't have the same ideas.
Not that I blame them. The town's cut off from the outside world. We're short on supplies. And a gang of armed Infected are out there circling.
Armed Infected?
Outlanders. All of them Infected, all of them armed. Most are Leithanian, but there are a few devils among them.
The term is 'Sarkaz,' please. I hope the meaning of the word is not lost on you. Do you have any other information about the armed Infected?
They're merely vultures waiting to pick our bones. And they're not the first.
Oh? So why didn't they immediately show up to plunder the town? Why are they just 'loitering' out there?
Just come out with it already.
Wolumonde's lack of sensitivity toward the Infected is understandable.
After all, it's a miracle Leithanien was letting infected people live alongside non-infected people for so long—
—But it's also an opportunity for bad actors to take advantage of such leniency.
What do you mean?
I told you, Rhodes Island isn't just a bunch of doctors. We're also experts at dealing with the Infected.
There are some areas... where I'd rather trust our own judgment.
For example... have you ever heard of the 'Reunion Movement?'
Before the Great Rift, I saw that name in the newspapers from time to time. Mostly reports from Ursus, but occasionally in Leithanien.
But to a small tourist town, it doesn't mean much. Folks around here would rather listen to the gossip from the next town over than official reports on faraway countries.
What are you getting at? You think Reunion has something to do with this?
No, I don't want to jump to conclusions. Let's leave it there for now.
Right... *Cough*. Either way, thanks for the info.
Speaking from experience, I don't think your side of the story will have much influence on our mission.
That's too bad. But honestly, I didn't expect much.
We may have to do some investigating around town. We need to find out what the deal with this 'disappearance' is...
Rhodes Island will demand the necessary compensation from the relevant people.
Er...
...Understood.
I've no reason to argue with that face of yours.
You can move about the town freely, as Dr. Atro's associates. You are honored guests of Wolumonde.
Ah! But of course! Please, make yourselves at home here. Upstairs is the Wolumonde government's finest parlor.
You must be tired from your long journey? Please, take a rest upstairs while we—
No need. I have something to attend to.
Can we start our investigation now?
We'll prepare the paperwork and notify the residents after the meeting. As you wish.
Thank you.
Suzuran, let's go.
Oh? Er, okay! See you soon, Wolumonders!
...*Sigh*.
How long can you keep them in the dark?
Did you see her expression?
I did.
You did. Rhodes Island may not be the type of company to try to extort us, but who knows what they might do to avenge a lost colleague?
The way I see it, it all ends the same.
But... but, it wasn't our fault. Shouldn't they be after the damned Infected that caused the fire?!
We have no proof.
But we can't just take the blame either!
We tolerated the Infected as much as we could, and how did they repay us? Ever since the Gendarmerie left, they've completely been running rampant!
...Indeed. As for the truth, truth is a fickle thing. See this match? Matches are more reliable than the truth.
The doctor's right. You really should quit. You've got a long life ahead of you.
They're 'operators.' I only listen to 'doctors.'
Touché...
Tatjana, keep an eye on our guests. Make sure they don't get into trouble.
Ah, yes, sir! Of course!
Her?
That one, what was the name... Folinic. Yes.
Do you know what Folinic's expression of pain and anger reminded me of?
Don't keep me waiting.
Tatjana. It made me think of her. Why didn't she show me a face like that?
Thor was very nearly her husband. You'd think she'd be a little sad.
3:59 P.M. \ Cloudy
Nomadic Town Wolumonde, Infected Quarter, Zwölftontechnik Strasse
Folinic... Are you angry?
The way they were acting, I know what it means. I know it all too well...
But... But Atro wouldn't get herself into trouble. She knows Leithanien better than any of us!
Yeah! I'm sure she's fine!
At least, I hope...
Isn't this place eerily quiet?
Yes. Stay close.
I'm seeing plenty of Infected among the citizenry, but that's not the main point.
I was thinking about it when we fought those rioters before. This residential district is pretty ordinary in design, isn't it?
They've just gathered up all the Infected to live together. There's nothing explicitly discriminatory about it.
Hm...
But the truth is they're still having their personal freedom restricted. And aside from a few remarkable Infected, most people live more or less like prisoners.
What they have is not a prison, but an entire street of cells with a dozen times more inmates.
Look here, how long do you think it's been since this bakery was open?
Ew... I think I saw an Originium slug crawling around... so unsanitary.
No one's been sweeping up the dust and leaves around the door. There's no functioning economy here. No daily life.
I understand they've fallen on hard times, but no one wants to buy or sell bread? There's no public transit? This isn't normal.
Maybe, maybe it's because of what happened before? Like the shop owner got scared off or something?
At least they have the right to live, they—
Shh! Miss Folinic...! Lower your voice!
I wasn't criticizing them. I was just talking about... those 'armed Infected.'
Do you think they're...
That policeman mentioned 'Sarkaz.' We need to look into that first.
If they do have anything to do with the 'armed Infected groups' we know, we'll have to tread carefully. Things here may be far more complicated than that policeman knows.
Really?
If the Infected are living well enough, it's only because the situation isn't desperate enough.
They must be hiding something from us.
Folinic?
Please don't worry. Atro is very smart.
You've been looking very scary since that meeting. But don't worry.
As long as we try really, really hard, we'll be able to handle this, just like the senior operators!
So can you relax for me? At least a bit?
Aw, is little Lisa comforting me again? Why's that?
Hehe, let go of all your troubles, big and small!
Doctors, please wait a moment—!
It's you.
Didn't I say we don't need any help? All we need is access...
I'm sorry. I know you said as much, Miss Folinic, but considering the circumstances, and your unfamiliarity with this place, we have to take precautions to guard against potential accidents.
...Miss Folinic?
Fine... The locals may be more welcoming if we have one of their own with us.
That's true! *Cough cough*! Zwölftontechnik Strasse was once Wolumonde's biggest Infected quarter...
And now, for certain reasons, it's become Wolumonde's number one protest district.
Then what do you plan to do now?
I don't think it's wise to go any deeper into this place... But if you want to learn more about the situation in Wolumonde, that is a good choice.
Let's just go for a stroll here. No need to rush things.
I'd be very happy if you were willing to tell me more about Dr. Atro.
You... really worry about Dr. Atro, don't you?
We went to school together. And she is one of my very few trusted friends. Not to mention an employee Rhodes Island can't afford to lose.
...I see.
Ah, please come this way. Schaukelplatz is just up ahead.
What was Atro doing here?
Um... Treating the local Infected, giving psychological counseling to those who were suffering.
Wolumonde was unable to avoid the Great Rift because of an error the Catastrophe Messengers made, and we suffered immensely for it.
Dr. Atro could have left, but she decided to stay. After Wolumonde moored here, she set up a temporary medical facility to better serve the Infected.
Dr. Atro... is their hero.
...She was just doing what she had to do.
She's always been better at practical work than rote learning.
Heh. I can see her face right now, looking so self-satisfied. That's Atro.
This is the first time you've smiled since coming to Wolumonde.
Is it?
You've been pretty scary all day long, Folinic.
Oh...
There's... a lot of people up ahead. Is that where everyone is?
Shouldn't you do something?
Peaceful assembly isn't a crime. That alone isn't grounds to detain them.
They're looking this way...
Why is everyone quiet all of a sudden?
No, this isn't right... Who's that talking to them?
Huh?
Behind them, in the very back, there's—
A Sarkaz...?
Sarkaz...?
Miss Folinic!
I know! They're coming at us! Hey, you, protect yourself!
O-okay!
—When did they appear?
I'm not sure. But the 'Gramophone' didn't start...
I told you before not to rely on those experimental automatic Arts devices.
If they have some educated Leithanians in that Infected group, they could have easily turned a machine against us.
Too late for that. The Infected quarter is crawling with them. They're whipping them up.
...The people here should be above that kind of provocation.
B-but there are more and more protesters every day. They're gathering up and—
What are their demands?
They want us to start supplying Zwölftontechnik Strasse again... and they want all quarantine measures on Zwölftontechnik Strasse lifted.
...Nonsense.
Of course, their main demand is that we give an appropriate response to the fire...
They don't give a rat's ass about the fire. Just you wait, everyone will forget what this was about soon enough.
What should we do? Rhodes Island is already here. It's only a matter of time before they find out.
We all know that. Why do you think I sent Tatjana with them?
Before we lay it all out for Rhodes Island, we have to be sure what kind of company they are. We have to know if they're Atro's kind of Infected, or 'the other' kind.
I'm not sure that's right... Won't things just get worse once they find out on their own what we're hiding?
Ha... Think what you will. If they're the type to turn the other cheek, that's what they'll do. You and I both know what's going on here in Wolumonde.
I know, but—
—Then don't try to play the good guy here. Things don't work out for the good guys. We need to focus on outcomes.
*Cough* *cough*... What about the real nasty ones? Like the devils?
They haven't made formal contact yet...
They're a bunch of vultures, stirring up our people, looking to divide and conquer. How trite of them.
They're playing it cool, probing our defenses. When they're sure there's no Gendarmerie garrisoned in Wolumonde...
Everyone here is ready to fight. And we have the L-44 'Gramophone' defense system.
But you're not Gendarmerie... *Sigh*, I'm just trying not to get anyone killed, *cough*— *cough* *cough* *hack*—
Take care of yourself. Wolumonde can't afford to lose you.
Really? I don't think it makes much difference...
Who else could keep Wolumonde's defenses up without a single Gendarmerie?
It's just stubborn blood. Leithanian nobles won't stoop to doing their own dirty work. Give them one more push and you'll see this place go to hell in a handbasket.
If we don't come up with something soon, this game is over.
We have to get back on our normal course before winter. Stay stuck in this wasteland and watch starvation and rioting destroy the town.
Maybe... Yes, you're right.
It's already getting cold, *cough*—
...I hope Wolumonde still has other options.
Hey! Where are you two from?!
Are you here to help those morons keep us locked up in here to die?!
Know your place!
Who were you talking to just now? What did you tell him?
Hah. You scared? You should be!
It's because you bureaucrats can't do shit that Wolumonde is in this mess!
Why don't you let us try running the show for a while?!
This isn't anyone's fault!
You're just gonna call it a Catastrophe and be done with it?! It's your fault we couldn't steer away from it in the first place!
...You're begging for a fight, Infected.
Have you been to any other cities? Have you seen how other Infected live?
Even I can see that Wolumonde is in a crisis, but the non-infected are going through the same tough times as you Infected. Shouldn't you be working together to get through this?
Huh? And who the hell are you?
I'm a Rhodes Island Operator, an Infected doctor. We help the Infected through crises, as long as they're not stirring up trouble for no reason.
G-good day. C-could you please stop resisting...?
Pfah! You're here to kick us around. If I let you, that is—!
You want us to work together? Why don't you ask the woman who started all this?
That...
Hey, you. You're infected, aren't you?
Mhm.
Nine-tailed Vulpos are pretty rare. Sooner or later, you'll end up just like us.
I don't think so... Everyone's so nice to me...
For now. But that won't last forever. Maybe not today, maybe not tomorrow, but soon enough.
If only Dr. Atro was here...
Hey! What are you doing?! Let go!
What did you just say?
Folinic, calm down!
Did you just say 'Dr. Atro?'
Where is she? Is she alright?
What do you mean where is she? The fire took everything from us!
They've been pushing us out since the Great Rift appeared. They're terrified!
They even rounded up the Infected who came here seeking refuge! Why? Because they have to control us, to lock us up like animals, all to satisfy their twisted idea of humanism!
That's not true... the Schultz isn't like that!
Feh. Whatever. If that's all it was, we'd be fine too...
At least we had Dr. Atro. At least we had each other. Even if we lost this quarter, we at least had a place to stay...
But they... they killed Dr. Atro, and our friends!
Because of that fire!
...Wh-what do you want?
...Atro should have survived that fire.
Huh?!
Oh, I get it. They lied to you. Of course. They lie to everyone.
It wasn't an accident. It was arson. It was murder. It was self-righteous moralizers showing their true colors.
I'm not your therapist. Let's cool the emotional venting—
Heh. You know what they didn't tell you?
Wait, stop!
...The day that fire happened, not a single person made it out of that tent!
You mean...?
I think you know whose side you should be on.
Enough.
Gah! Take it easy already!
...Behave yourself. You're under arrest.
You're no Gendarmerie! None of you are! There's no law here! All we got is leftovers from the settlers!
The Schultz will decide your fate. I'm sorry.
Hmph. You wouldn't dare kill me. You wouldn't risk a mass Infected uprising!
I guess I'll just wait for you to exile me to the wasteland so I can join up with the—
...You and your friends are convinced that we are the ones behind all this.
But we're all fellow citizens of Wolumonde, and we're all a part of this town together. We've lived in peace for so many years—
Why would you trust some the word of some strange outsiders over ours?
What have we done wrong?
...Because you are not one of the Winterwisp. Nor are you Infected. You're just a sheltered little girl.
You forget what it means to be Infected. You forget what Oripathy is. I know better than you. It's that simple.
Here! Tatjana is here!
And Rhodes Island! Are you two okay?
Yes, we're fine!
Take them away, please.
Ugh. I have to see Severin again...
We'll make sure they don't do anything else. But Miss Tatjana, what of the two from Rhodes Island?
I will take care of them.
Very well. Then, we'll go on ahead.
Now can you give me an explanation?
Miss Folinic...
I... I will tell you everything.
I don't know what Schultz Severin and the councilors are thinking. But I believe... Miss Folinic and Miss Suzuran, I believe you deserve to know the truth.
Considering how you just helped me...
You're wasting time. I just want to know... is she alive?
I... I don't know...
You don't know?!
Please, Miss Folinic.
Miss Tatjana... why are you lying to me?
None of us are really ready to accept it ourselves.
Please understand, we don't know what Rhodes Island is like. We had to be sure we could trust—
Even if we were ruthless, cold-blooded corporate raiders out to make everything worse for Wolumonde, that's no excuse for you to deceive our people!
I'm sorry... I really am...
We were planning to tell you the truth...! We, we've been doing everything we can to investigate the Infected outside the town!
But I know. I know that's all just for our own sake. It's no excuse...
Exactly right. You're making excuses. But... it's fine.
All I want... is the truth. Rhodes Island won't abandon you. We wouldn't look past a town suffering a crisis like this.
That would make Atro very unhappy. She's such a baby whenever she gets sad. And I hate seeing her cry.
It's not too late. For either of us.
Helping the Infected and solving their problems is what being a Rhodes Island Operator is all about.
Miss Tatjana, there's so much we can do if we just trust each other!
Thanks.
Before the Catastrophe, the Schultz asked Dr. Atro if she would leave with the refugees...
Dr. Atro said the same thing—'Rhodes Island won't abandon you.'
Please come with me.
This... this is...
Rhodes Island supply crates, yes. With dates and serial numbers.
The last time we made contact, it was me who...
Miss Folinic...
What caused the fire?
An accident... At least, that's what we decided at first, but...
Dr. Atro would never be so careless as to leave flammables lying around. All the signs point to this being caused by someone's Arts.
We couldn't figure out where the fire started. The tent's metal support frame melted, and the bodies...
Oh...
It's fine. Continue.
Judging from the remains, the victims couldn't even leave the tent. There was no struggle. And of course, they were completely unrecognizable.
I don't think an ordinary fire could just incinerate Dr. Atro and her patients like that.
It would have to be some form of Originium Arts that could achieve such high temperatures so quickly...
How many of them were Infected?
Hm? Oh... three from our village: Kevin, Biederman, and Eckehard were receiving treatment. And there was also...
Thor... Thorwald.
He was the only son of the Schultz. And my friend. He wasn't Infected. He just went there to help, out of the kindness of his own heart.
Just let it out, Miss Tatjana. You need to get it off your chest.
Huh?
I can sense the pain here.
Here... there was a lot of discontent here. Much joy, much pain. But it's all gone now.
Is that... is that an Originium Art?
No. I've just seen this kind of thing so many times. Everyone wants to keep their feelings bottled up inside. But it's not healthy.
Maybe I'm wrong. But if you treat yourself like that, you'll worry the people who care about you, no?
You should cry. Cry it all out. No one will judge you.
You're right. Thank you. I already knew that, in a way, but for now I can't... I just can't let it go.
Hm—
Don't. Don't look at me, Lisa. I get it.
Hm!
*Sigh*... Now, I'm sorry!
Huh? What's that about...?
...I thought I had prepared myself mentally back on the Rhodes Island landship. Atro was a very dedicated doctor. She always reported in on time.
Of course I know what it means to lose contact with an operator. Rhodes Island has said goodbye to so many. I just couldn't get myself to believe it.
I... I let my emotions get the better of me. And I'm sorry.
But we're not the only ones to lose somebody to this act of murder.
Schultz Severin found the remains of eight individuals.
Since there are no Infected experts in our town, we can only hastily bury them...
We can't let them stay with Wolumonde. They'll be buried in the wasteland outside the town. And when Wolumonde leaves, they'll lose their hometown forever.
Of course... in keeping with our traditions... we should do better than that, at least...
This sort of thing happens a lot.
You said there were eight?
Yes. The five I just mentioned, and the others should be some wanderers who came seeking treatment.
When the Great Rift came, Wolumonde didn't escape unscathed, and of course many merchants and travelers nearby got caught up in the Catastrophe.
Things were bad for Wolumonde, but there was nothing else those people could do but chase down the town slowly crawling across the wasteland.
The look of the refugees coming in started to change... But Dr. Atro treated everyone equally. She even rescued some of the Infected. At least the ones who would accept the help.
That means one non-infected and seven Infected.

What about the Infected remains? Within minutes to hours of the cessation of vital signs, depending on the rate of Originium assimilation in the body, there is a risk of secondary spread. It's very dangerous if not handled appropriately.
Oh, right, well, the Schultz decided that because they were only lightly infected, and with the rapid incineration of tissue, there wasn't much risk.
Didn't you say you had no Infected experts?
Oh, Schultz Severin just said he was going off of experience, and couldn't be absolutely sure.
Experience, huh...? What kind of job gives you experience handling Infected corpses...?
But anyway, if he can keep his cool even after losing his own flesh and blood, maybe he takes his job more seriously than I thought.
I would like to inspect the Infected remains... I need to see Atro myself.
Yes, of course. I'll explain it to the Schultz. But most of the remains are charred so badly that we can't tell who was who.
No problem, I—
Ah—!
Lisa?
That badge...
Wow... it's Atro's employee badge. It survived...
Oh. Right.
She always complained it got in the way of her work. Isn't that funny? Just wear it on your neck or your chest. How could it get in the way...?
Let's go. I want to see her...
What was that sound?
It's coming from the town!
M-Miss Folinic! Someone's coming!
That symbol. It's... Rhodes Island...? When did you get here...?
And with your outfit... you're no ordinary Infected.
Are you Leithanian? Or the Sarkaz they were talking about?
They're some armed Infected partisans who showed up outside the town recently. It looks like a group of Sarkaz make up the core, but they've taken in a lot of our local Infected.
The call themselves... the 'Mudrock Squad.'
I can see the Rathaus spire from here! Thank goodness, it looks like everyone's safe!
Lisa, hurry!
Okay!
Wh-what happened? Is there a fire?
My sons haven't come back. Has anyone seen them?!
Where are the Gendarmerie?! Where is Severin?! Isn't he supposed to be a hero? And he's missing at a time like this?!
Everyone's panicking!
But it looks like they haven't attacked the residential areas. Someone must be leading them!
Everyone's been on edge for far too long. That explosion was all they needed to snap—
Over here! We have to make sure the Rathaus is secure!
Look! There's a lot of them! Hurry!
Hah?!
Not bad for a man your age. Must be nice to live without the constant torment of Oripathy, huh?
Sure... If I gave up smoking in my younger days, you and your friends would be sharing a jail cell by now.
Get out of here. Out of respect for you as my fellow townsfolk, I won't—
Hah! Look at the mouth on this one! You think we don't know you're the last cop in Wolumonde?
You...
Severin! The jig is up. Let us into the Rathaus so we can run our town already.
What do you plan to do?
Hah?!
Settle down. Truce. I'm going to smoke a cigarette while you tell me what exactly you want from this town. Go ahead.
Maybe Wolumonde can consider your demands.
What are you trying to pull?
Look, I'm putting down my sword. I think we can come to terms here. No need to fight.
Hah. Now you decide to be reasonable?
Hand over the murderer. And abandon your prejudices.
...I want to go home. And I want my job back. Simple enough?
I just need you to fill out a report, and submit to regular physicals. Simple enough.
That's it?! You walled us in and left us to die!

All that's left of my block is a heap of earthquake rubble. It's all Originium clusters that could blow at any moment. You evacuated all the non-infected and we're running out of supplies. Is anyone coming to help us?
Oh right, I almost forgot. Because you suck at your job, Wolumonde is heading straight to hell, and we're just the first ones to be thrown under the bus.
You still think you're so bright, hiding the truth to 'maintain order?'
...*Sigh*.
Looks like my matches got wet. I guess I don't have a light. Let's get back to it.
Wha?! Hand-to-hand?
Sure. Just a quick whiff of tobacco is enough to pump me up.
But, I'm sorry to say, we may be about to have an 'accident' here.
Damn it, let go! Are... Are you trying to kill me? Gah—!
No, just a 'slip' of the hand—
Schultz!
Hm?
Um...
Finished. There were only a few of them, and the rest ran off, leaving this poor fella alone.
Did you kill him?
I don't think so.
It would be nice if the good doctors could wake him up.
He's breathing!
Let's make sure we're saving lives now.
I'll have words for you soon, 'Schultz.'
Sure. I'm all ears, Miss.
Sir...
I told you before, I'm not 'Sir.' I'm not your Schultz when we're alone. I'm Thor's father, and your uncle.
No, Sir, you just... you were going to kill that Infected man.
I won't deny it.
That's only going to make things worse! And you can't attack a member of the Wolumonde family so lightly!
Even if he told the militants that we're defenseless?
You know what this means, Tatjana.
You should have a heart, at least—
Can't always make the right choice, no matter how much you might want to. Not unless you're omniscient.
Objectively speaking, if a prophet or a banker baron told me just a few deaths would bring peace back to Wolumonde...
I'd do it. No matter who had to die.
...Even if...
No. Matter. Who.
...At least he managed to hold on to his life. That's good enough.
Let's get back to Rhodes Island. Did you tell them the truth? Did you already take them to the camp? That girl had a different look in her eye.
You already knew...?
I was pretty sure. For what it's worth, I never saw the point in hiding it from them. But keeping it under wraps myself calmed a lot of people down.
What's your read on them?
I think they'll help Wolumonde. They're the same kind of people as Dr. Atro.
*Cough* *cough*, *hack*. And you think they speak for all of Rhodes Island?
They're the ones they sent. So I guess they should be decently representative?
Truth be told, if every Rhodes Island operator we ran into turned out to be a good person, that would disturb me a lot more.
I'm thinking back... to a certain sniper. She was from Rhodes Island...
The one who came with Dr. Atro and left shortly thereafter?
I hope she didn't get swallowed up by the Catastrophe...
Wh-what? Another attack?
At this point, every bandit for miles must know we have no Gendarmerie here.
So what can we do? Get down on our knees and beg for mercy? Or fight to the bitter end?
No, those explosions are coming from... underground?!
Severin! They're over at the workshops! They came through a tunnel on the next street over!
They crippled the main power plant!
Look at it. A beautiful, empty town.
Wolumonde. Moon of the Winterwisp Mountains. Yes, truly worthy of its name.
A moon? Pfah!
Four of our people died in that fire.
And they have the gall to try to pin it on the Infected? What's next? Where do they go from here?
Do they just expect us to turn the other cheek?
You're quite worked up. Try to relax. You don't usually talk like that.
We should level this place! Damned Wolumonders! Damned immigrants!
No.
Mudrock! You and your Sarkaz could do it!
No.
You're right... Who knows the pain of losing one's home better than the Sarkaz?
Even in Leithanien, where the Infected are supposed to be able to do what they can with what they have, there is no place for the Sarkaz.
...But, the cupcakes that nice granny gave us were delicious. Just the right amount of sweet.
Huh?
So... We wait. The only thing we're here to destroy is the murderer. We can't lose control of our hate. Or else...
What separates us from what we were before?
How...?
It's... awful...
A fire. It's already been dealt with. And some time has passed. But...
Here. Rhodes Island supply crates. With dates, serial numbers, everything.
There's an outline in the ash. Looks like there was something rectangular sitting on the box?
Maybe Atro's ID badge. She didn't like to wear it.
Operator Click, Operator Ayerscarpe, our situation has changed.
Y-yeah, I know already!
Since you and Ayers rescued me from the mountain, I'm more than glad to help!
Don't call me Ayers... *Sigh*. What has Leonhardt been telling you...?
The Catastrophe was immensely destructive. Wolumonde will not be in better shape now. What were their Catastrophe Messengers doing?
Things wouldn't have gotten so bad if Leonhardt or Earthspirit was there...
No point thinking about that now... we should prepare for the worst.
Any objections to setting up camp outside the town?
None whatsoever!
I am after all a professional Catastrophe Messenger bodyguard. And this is the 'wunderkind' who nearly got herself killed shooting videos deep in the Winterwisp Mountains.
Why are you still rubbing salt into my wounded pride?!
Just kidding. But... GreyThroat wouldn't want us to storm the town and kick in the doors of the mayor's office, would she?
Sorry. Please be patient. I have to confirm what happened to Atro.
I'll be right back.
...Famine?
Yes. Famine ruins everyone.
I know what you're going to say. A town the size of Wolumonde should be self-sufficient.
The day the Great Rift happened, there wasn't so much Originium breaking through the ground, carving the land up, ruining farms and granaries.
We've been very lucky, in fact. There haven't been any explosions in the town center or any of the very populated areas. But even so, the losses have been severe...
That, on top of an inopportune season. We're well into autumn and we're on a course for the Winterwisp Mountains. Winter's coming early this year.
We haven't announced it to the public yet, as to not cause a panic.
And then we were stabbed in the back by our own people, unwitting as they may be.
What do we do then... about the traitors?
Tatjana took the young people to survey the situation. No one could reach the power plant without a guide.
Each district should have its own auxiliary hauling equipment. What's the situation?
Too slow. At best an eighth the speed of the other towns. We wouldn't get out of the mountains before winter.
We can't let anything happen to the granary. Send more men.
Men...? Even if we're talking old folks and children, anyone who's willing to carry a weapon is already...
But they wouldn't go so far as to attack the granary. That's tantamount to killing every last person in Wolumonde!
You give them too much credit.
Um...
Go ahead.
Zwölftontechnik Strasse, the quarantine zone, is it as bleak as it is because...?
Would you believe me if I told you we had to cut down on rations entirely because of the Infected population?
No, it doesn't matter if you believe me or not. Would they believe me?
They would tear down the granary doors and count every last grain before they believed anything that came out of us.
So, do we let them tear it down?
I wouldn't mind a little open honesty if they really just wanted to take a look. But not everyone's going to let it end there.
Even without an official announcement, anyone smart enough would be able to figure it out with a calendar and a look at our route that Wolumonde is bound for a terrible winter famine.

They stirred the people up into a frenzy, they looted. They're just thinking of themselves, making sure they can survive, whether or not it means someone else has to starve. To put it bluntly, they want an excuse to steal.
I believe Rhodes Island has the expertise of dealing with the Infected, like you say. Dr. Atro's professionalism and your combat skills proved that much.
But...
Is Oripathy the only problem in the world?
Even at a time like this, I have to ask after Atro.
That was a murder.
Everyone out! The Rhodes Islanders have something important to discuss with the Schultz!
Severin.
I know.
Then, I leave it to you.
I guess this isn't a topic you can put on the agenda?
Should I have added 'murder' as 'old business' or 'new business?'
It's all a consequence of your coverup. No, no need for formalities. I'm disgusted with you.
Rhodes Island Operator Atro was murdered in the course of her duties here in Wolumonde. And I need answers.
And, according to a particular Infected resident, they are also hunting this 'murderer.'
Maybe the killer is one of those Infected. Like when a thief cries out 'thief!' Oldest trick in the book.
Maybe...
But before we discuss the murderer, I'd like—
Stop. You don't have to say it.
I do have enough of a conscience left that your request would wound me.
But Wolumonde needs Rhodes Island's help to get out of this mess. And there's no point in making things hard for you anymore.
Come with me.
What is this place...
Sorry about the... hasty handling. But they're mostly Infected. We had to put them somewhere underground to keep the others from panicking.
...It's cold.
I make sure to keep the refrigeration units running.
Impressive Arts. As to be expected from a Leithanian officer.
Eight victims.
Four from Wolumonde, Dr. Atro, and three Infected outlanders.
Including your son... Mr. Thorwald.
Tatjana told you that too, huh?
It's true.
Because I wasn't there...
Here you are... Atro.
You... You really gave me the runaround here... Stupid cat.
Incredible... how can she tell...?
(Could we... give them some space?)
Alright.
As strong as she is, I forgot she's just another girl trying her hardest to move on. Same age as Tatjana too.
Tatjana is... holding up.
I know.
Hey! I told you to quit smoking!
Maybe...
Smoking will only make your bronchitis worse!
Are... are you...?
You got it, little fox. You're sharper than I expected.
But do me a favor and keep it to yourself.
Do you... feel any better, Miss Folinic?
Yes.
Quite impressive, exhibiting such resilience at your age.
I know my eyes are swollen. Are you mocking me?
You lost a good friend and you're still keeping your calm. What could I possibly mock you about?
...Things aren't over yet.
You're right, it isn't. Now, I hope we can work together.
What is this?
The Arts core of the L-44 'Gramophone' autonomous defense device. To put it bluntly, this is your murder weapon.
Of course, civilians aren't allowed to possess destructive power like this. But any educated Leithanian would know how to drive this core.
Overloading, heating, and igniting are all features of our town's defensive equipment. Originium Arts engineering is complex. Not everyone can use it.
So this is it. Someone used this to kill Atro...
We have to find the murderer.
And then what?
And then... I'll make them pay.
How about that. You move past restlessness and sorrow, and you choose revenge.
Miss Folinic... we shouldn't...
I know.
Of course I know. As a Rhodes Island Operator, as Dr. Kal'tsit's student, I should know better than anyone.
But the murderer, and the murderer alone, must face justice!
What did Atro do? She was helping the Infected! What did she do to deserve this?!
At least...
At least let me ask them... I have to know why...
I understand.
Schultz Severin, Miss Suzuran, Miss Fol—
Oh... Miss Folinic has already seen...
They had their time together. You look flustered. Did something happen?
Yes. During our inspection, a fight broke out between the Infected and the non-infected...
Again.
But this time... the councilors weren't even able to calm them down. They're demanding a declaration of war against the armed Infected.
People must be starting to understand what it means to have our town's movement basically crippled... They can't hold it in any longer.
But there's more... Many of the residents who were working with the Infected were themselves uninfected. They...
I already figured as much.
Huh?
The Winterwisps. Those damned savages. They're back to haunt Wolumonde again.
Mama? Mama, where are you going?
To the highlands? The flowery highlands?

Then... you just want a riot.

*Sigh*, sometimes I can't tell if they're so corrupt that they don't care about the plight of the people, or if they're just so far off the ground that they can't even see it.
B-but that will kill a lot of people...
A lot of people will die if we do nothing too. When things get a little worse for Wolumonde, there will be plenty of reasons for this already troubled town to implode.
Not to mention, there's a rather unusual group of Infected floating around Wolumonde. They're armed, they're rebels, and they're bound to bring a fight.
And so, it's up to us to carve a path that gets as few people killed as possible.
Don't worry. We don't have to the dirty work ourselves. Maybe a jealous youth, maybe a stubborn cobbler. Then, all it takes is a spark to light the fire...
Hey!
This is an opportunity to atone for your sins. Lead the rebellion.
Damn it... How can you just hide behind a curtain and watch innocent people suffer?!
Hey, this is an easy moral choice to make. I won't even hesitate. Not for a second.
Unless I'm killing all the good guys to save all the bad guys. But lord knows who's good and who's bad.
What's more, don't get me wrong. I'm not going to hide behind a curtain and pay lip service to sacrifice when I'm not willing to die myself. What kind of conspirator would I be?
I know the deal. I deserve to die. This will be my one, final sacrifice. I'll give myself closure.
I'm going to die in this riot. I've earned it.
Are you crazy?!
Oh, right, and if you agree to participate, you're going to die too.

But don't worry. But any objective measure, you will have helped Wolumonde. You'll have atoned for your sins, and subjectively paid for your crimes with your own death. Two for one. Pretty great deal, huh?
And like I said, you won't even have to kill the Infected yourself. Your biggest crime will be 'conspiracy' or 'aiding and abetting.' It's nothing.
Everyone sins. For some, it's a sin of omission. So many people sin that way...
They're all so nice until they realize it. That's when they turn desperate.
So I'll make it a lot easier and just kill myself.
Think about it.
It's all about saving lives. More lives.
Wait!
I open the door, but no one is there.
There are ice crystals on the ground. It looks like it wasn't easy to get here. His plan in the nearby city had mostly failed.
But, I don't care much about his earlier plans. He's not going after Rhodes Island on his own. But he is right.
Wolumonde is very unstable right now.
All about... saving more lives.
Why do I have to do this?
This wasn't my fault in the first place. And Dr. Atro is good to me. I don't need to atone for some dumb hypothetical...
I... I don't need to. Right. There won't be any uprising. Wolumonde will get through this.
I have to help Dr. Atro move boxes tomorrow... and I'll tell her about it tomorrow. As long as there's no mass uprising, everything he said was just bullshit.
But if... if these people do start some kind of civil war over famine and cold...I have to...
No, at least she can be spared. There's no need to make a sacrifice of Dr. Atro.
Right.
Oh. Right.
So you didn't leave. You're like a ghost.
If that Infected doctor were to die... What would become of your interpersonal relationships, and your work?
If you want to stir up trouble, you can do it without Atro's death.
Ooh... will you help me? You really don't want anything to happen to that doctor, do you?
But I will warn you one more time.
The death of the Infected is not something I would arrange. It is just a consequence of which I am absolutely certain. Sooner or later, someone will do something about them.
Don't look at me like that. It's not like you can't save that doctor, if you really want to.
How are you so certain that things are going to go in the worst possible direction, Thor? You've always been such a nice boy.
Because it has to happen. It won't be the first time either. Before, I just watched. But now I'm in it.
You should talk to your father, and the little girl with you. You shouldn't be so...
What a humanitarian you are. I suggest you glue yourself to that doctor.
Anyway... you can underestimate me. I went to great lengths just to escape my pursuers.
Heh. Covering myself with ice Arts in the freezing snow, nearly killing myself with my own Originium Arts.
But don't underestimate the violence brewing in Wolumonde. The worst possible violence.
Oh... are you going to kill me?
No, you're not. When the time comes, in order to get one side to compromise as quickly as possible, you will offer to help the other side.
I don't need to, Thorwald. No matter who you have in mind, you're now an accomplice to a capital crime.
Well... You can watch what happens then. But not now.
And I have you to thank for that. My original idea was just to hand 'this' over to some indignant non-infected and wait for something to happen.
That's the core of an Arts unit. Where did you get it?
The Gramophone. That Gramophone. You see it?
When it's done, I'll be waiting there for you.
At my grave. Ha ha... it's so close.
You're scared.
I'm always scared.
Mudrock, they're hunting down the last fugitives.
They could have just left the fugitives to fend for themselves in the mountains. Capturing them will save their lives.
I can't tell if they're being cruel or kind.
The ones who didn't follow us are not our problem, but...
Try to avoid the search parties. We need to get out of here before winter.
It's already cold.
And go where?
We've been wandering for so long. Where does it end? It definitely won't end well.
Then, let's go home.
We go back and find a quiet patch of woods. And we survive.
What about our fight?
For now... we live.
To live, just to live, we are fighting the world every day.
We're going home? To what home?
Kazdel.
Huh...
But what about us? We're not Sarkaz. That's...
You are companions of Sarkaz.
There may be no place for Sarkaz outside of Kazdel. But in Kazdel, there is a place for you.
Not because of tolerance. But because there is... nothing there.
Kazdel... is not a city. Not a country. Technically, it is just a region.
A land of exiles where we are not allowed to build homes.
Kazdel... is where the homeless go. A land of rootless people.
It is where you belong.
Thank you.
Maybe we can live in peace in Kazdel. It's full of Infected Sarkaz.
Leithanien's leniency toward the Infected is ultimately just a product of the popularity of bloodlines and Originium Arts. It doesn't mean they truly accept you.
As soon as the mask came off, the very first to be ostracized were still the Infected.
It's not just Oripathy. This rich country has forgotten all the depressed corners of our world.
A tragedy like Wolumonde's is just a mild annoyance for the aristocrats. It's a topic of conversation for the bankers and court artists at tea time.
We can't accept it. We won't.
So we leave. We'll go to Kazdel. Let's see your hometown.
Mm...
Will Leithanien come after us?
Maybe. Regardless of what happened before, a group of Infected Sarkaz showed up and destroyed a town. Piss-poor optics.
Severin knows the truth. Would he try to frame us?
He would.
It's just the way he does things. Based on the information he has, he will do what causes the least amount of damage to his town.
Then we have to get out of here quickly, or all of Leithanien is going to come down on us.
Yes. So we must figure out a route soon... Oh...
Sorry. Wait a moment. I have to go somewhere.
Very quick.
So that's where you were.
Operator Folinic.
No, don't get up. I won't get too close to you.
You're waiting for someone, aren't you?
Yes.
I wasn't on the Rhodes Island landship when the Reunion incident happened. You... changed a lot.
You're very different from what the rumors say about you.
Rumors?
I heard you fought alongside an elite operator to solve a lot of the problems in Lungmen.
...a big, dumb, reckless cat who gets blood everywhere.
And we weren't able to solve... anything.
...Thank you.
I have to thank you again. I don't know how things would have turned out without you and Lisa.
Rhodes Island, Severin, even Reunion. No one wanted it to come to this.
Everyone was fighting to stop the tragedy, but we were powerless.
Atro, she...
Don't push yourself to mourn anything or anyone. It's over now.
Just pray for the dead to rest in peace.
We failed to bring the killer to justice. Not only that, we failed to protect the town Atro gave her life for.
She would have blamed me, wouldn't she?
Only you would blame yourself. Atro was a cheerful, open-minded woman.
She would slap you on the shoulder and get drunk with you.
Heh... you're right. Seems like you knew her well?
I know a certain 'elite operator' she liked to get drunk with.
We're not the only ones who have given up on chasing all this.
You came.
Here, freshly picked wildflowers...
You should be aware of the situation at hand. The cleanup mission is nearing completion and Rhodes Island will be leaving the area soon to try to contact Resource Dispatch for assistance.
But when that happens...
No one will speak for us. I know.
I don't blame Severin. This is the only thing he can tell the nobles, or his people will be the ones to suffer.
We too will leave here soon. It's just that, some of our people died here... I don't want to leave without saying goodbye.
...Eight Infected.
But not, the number of casualties in Wolumonde is hundreds of times greater than that.
Sorry.
No... it's not your fault. I wasn't blaming you.
I'm just reflecting on it. Thinking about how it came to this.
Of course, I know the world is not fair. Good things don't come to good people. But also... rarely have I ever seen such self-destructiveness.
Fomented anger and lust for revenge nearly destroyed everything... even me.
You're still blaming yourself.
No... I've calmed down past that. I'm only reevaluating, maybe what I thought was 'done wrong' just 'couldn't be done.'
Everything from Rhodes Island, everything Dr. Kal'tsit taught me... I just couldn't do it.
No one could.
I stopped them from crossing the line, but one or two stops can't quench their vengeful rage.
So... things are the way they are.
What are you going to do next? 'Reunion' is still a big problem for many people. You should let go of that symbol.
You're right. But 'Reunion' is not dead. And this news has spread among the Infected in Leithanien.
The fire of resistance continues to burn... twisted and distorted as it is. We must take a moment to catch our breaths.
There are too many in my squad with no combat experience. I can't decide their fate. They... perhaps they just want to live in peace.
Say... Rhodes Island, have you ever heard the name 'Big Bob?'
No.
If there's a connection, I could search the archives.
No. There isn't. He's just a friend of mine...
Reunion? Or Sarkaz?
Reunion. He is an experienced bounty hunter.
They are not warriors, so they had a reason to get out... They should free themselves from oppression and seek a place for themselves among the Columbian pioneers.
Ah, come to think of it, they have hops.
What?
Have you ever seen hops? How does it work? You crush it up into beer?
Um, it's a fine plant... it has something to do with the beer foam. Um, I think it prolongs the antiseptic—
No. I don't really have to explain this, do I? I'm not that familiar with it.
Let's just say, it's very handy.
I see. Lucky.
Hops... foam... beer...
Hey, don't get lost thinking about beer. We're still enemies here.
We may never be able to bridge our differences.
Oh... sorry.
We are warriors. Sarkaz are warriors.
I have to say... it's hard to feel your warrior spirit from your words and actions.
We had no choice.
If we have any demands, any convictions, anything to protect, we can only fight.
There are other ways to solve problems.
Then... what of our fallen companions? If we stand peacefully, will the murderers open their homes and cities to us?
They won't.
We can only fight on... to help our fellow Infected.
'Our fellow Infected.'
Where are you going?
Kazdel.
Kazdel is not a good place.
We're not good guys, Rhodes Island.
Any real 'good place' is closed to us.
You have another choice, Mudrock.
No. Not now.
These Leithanians, these Sarkaz, they all have their own ideas.
The resentment is still there. Nothing has made it easier to trust each other. Only the will to live as one ever held them all together. That was the only bond.
How sad.
No, Rhodes Island Swallow. Maybe we'll see each other again someday... to fight for the Infected.
You're not the first one from Reunion to call me that.
Is that so...
After I leave here, we are, as before, enemies.
Otherwise, Leithanien will suspect you. And you won't have an explanation.
So... don't get too close to your enemy.
I came only to offer a bouquet. And I did. So... it's time to go.
Good luck.
If you have anything else to say, now is the time.
I don't.
I feel like I've been living a nightmare. And watching him walk away, it's like finally waking up.
It's... over.
I'm not very good at comforting people, but—
I know you're not the one who should be comforting me. Sorry.
We just have to let go of the grief. It's not a good thing. It's a compromise that lets us keep living.
How long did it take you to come to terms with it?
A long time. Long enough to lose even more.
What now?
We go back to Rhodes Island.
We... take Atro home.
